In the meantime, approving/allow-listing PowerShell for the session (or splitting the command) should unblock you.

issues.title: How to access the already running process in the container programmatically?

issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for trying the WSLC Containers SDK, @Sonix. Today the SDK's process APIs are oriented around launching a new process inside the container; there isn't yet a first-class way to attach to the container's entrypoint process (the `CMD ["someapp"]` PID 1) and re-bind its stdin/stdout/stderr after the fact. Could you clarify what you ultimately need: (a) live stdout/stderr streaming of the entrypoint, or (b) interactive stdin as well?
